source.name: gstack by Garry Tan — GStack Browser
source.license_notes: MIT — architecture patterns extracted and generalized

goal: Enable AI agents to perform real browser QA with sub-second latency by running a long-lived Chromium daemon with persistent sessions, cookie import, and security-isolated remote access.
when_to_use: Web UI functional testing with agent-driven browser commands
Projects where cold-starting a browser per command is too slow
inputs_required: Cookie import configuration (optional, for login-state testing)
steps: Start persistent browser daemon (auto-spawn on first use)
Import cookies from real browser if login state needed
Execute QA commands via HTTP POST to localhost daemon
Generate QA report with pass/fail/skip and P0/P1/P2 issues
commands_or_apis: POST /tunnel/start — enable remote pair-agent access (scoped token only)
snapshot, type, click, screenshot, console, network commands
verification: Browser daemon responds to health check
QA commands execute within 200ms after first call
Screenshots captured for all test scenarios
failure_modes: Daemon idle timeout kills session mid-QA
Cookie import fails for browser with encrypted storage
ngrok tunnel exposes sensitive paths if misconfigured
Windows Bun Playwright pipe transport incompatibility
safety_notes: Use dual-listener architecture for remote access: separate local port (full access) from tunnel port (allowlisted commands only)
Never place root token on tunnel-accessible endpoints
Cookie import only from local browser — never shared environments
Prompt injection defense: ML classifier + LLM voting + canary tokens
handoff_report: Any CAPTCHA encountered and resolution status

quality.notes: Architecture pattern extracted from GStack Browser v1.21.1.0. The daemon model, dual-listener tunnel architecture, and domain skills mechanism are directly applicable to any agent-driven browser QA system.

retrieval.ai_summary: Persistent Chromium daemon architecture for agent-driven browser QA: long-lived browser with HTTP API, persistent cookies/sessions, sub-second commands, cookie import from real browsers, CAPTCHA handoff mechanism, dual-listener security isolation for remote access, and prompt injection defense (ML+LLV+canary).
